title: Data Mining for Decision Making - Week 1

questions.question: What is the key assumption behind the Naïve Bayes classifier?
options: Attributes are conditionally independent given the class
questions.answer: Attributes are conditionally independent given the class

questions.question: Which probability is estimated directly from the training data in Naïve Bayes?
Why is Laplace smoothing used in Naïve Bayes classification?
options: To avoid zero probability for unseen attribute values
questions.answer: To avoid zero probability for unseen attribute values

questions.question: Which probability is called the prior probability in Naïve Bayes?
Which of the following correctly represents Bayes’ theorem?
How are numeric attributes commonly handled in Naïve Bayes?
In Naïve Bayes, which probability is maximized to assign a class label?
If an attribute value never appears with a class in training data, Naïve Bayes will assign:
Which of the following is an example of a categorical attribute?
A decision list is best described as:
options: An ordered sequence of IF–THEN rules
questions.answer: An ordered sequence of IF–THEN rules

questions.question: Which of the following best defines data mining?
options: Discovering patterns and knowledge from data
questions.answer: Discovering patterns and knowledge from data

questions.question: Which task is NOT a data mining task?
Which attribute type requires probability density estimation in Naïve Bayes?

Which section of an ARFF file contains the actual dataset values?
